target_scope: aconcagua – Astro 5 static marketing site (repo root)

entry_points.description: ThemeSlider rendered inside .nav-right between LangSwitcher and CTA button
ThemeSlider rendered inside .nav-overlay for mobile users

affected_files.purpose: New range-input component with moon/sun icon labels, scoped styles, and module script that sets --theme-mix and data-theme on <html>
Add --theme-mix CSS custom property; rewrite all theme-variant color tokens as color-mix() expressions; collapse [data-theme='light'] color overrides into :root
Restore --theme-mix from localStorage('theme-mix') before CSS renders to prevent flash-of-wrong-theme; includes backward-compat fallback for old 'theme' key
Replace import ThemeToggle + both <ThemeToggle /> usages with import ThemeSlider + <ThemeSlider />

phases.name: CSS Token Refactor – color-mix() Interpolation
phases.tdd: RED: add --theme-mix var and test that mid-value 0.5 does nothing yet; GREEN: rewrite all theme tokens as color-mix(); REFACTOR: remove redundant [data-theme='light'] overrides

phases.name: FOWT Prevention Update
phases.tdd: RED: confirm flash occurs without the updated script; GREEN: update BaseLayout.astro inline script; REFACTOR: test backward compat with old theme key

phases.name: ThemeSlider Component
phases.tdd: RED: placeholder renders without build errors; GREEN: full implementation with range input, icons, script, styles; REFACTOR: cross-browser slider thumb styling and accessibility

phases.name: Integration & Exposure (Glue)
phases.tdd: N/A – wiring step; verified by manual test script
mandatory_changes: Nav.astro line 3: import ThemeSlider instead of ThemeToggle

key_design_decisions.rationale: Native CSS solution – no runtime JS computation per frame; just set one custom property and all tokens update. Supported in Chrome 111+, Firefox 113+, Safari 16.4+ (2026 audience baseline).
key_design_decisions.alternatives_considered: JS-computed interpolation (more complex, more JS per input event)

key_design_decisions.rationale: Components like ThemeToggle (and potentially ThemeSlider itself) use :global([data-theme='light']) for icon visibility (binary flip at midpoint). Retaining data-theme avoids a larger refactor of component-scoped styles.

box-shadow shorthand strings cannot be interpolated via color-mix() directly. These are visually acceptable as a binary flip; the user perceives shadows as less critical than background/text color gradients.
Granular numeric value allows exact restoration of any slider position. Backward-compat fallback for old binary 'theme' key included in both FOWT script and component init.
key_design_decisions.migration: If 'theme-mix' absent, check 'theme': dark→0, light→100, else use OS preference

risks.mitigation: Target audience is 2026; caniuse shows 95%+ global support. Add @supports fallback that keeps existing [data-theme] binary system for unsupported browsers.
File can be deleted after Nav.astro is updated; Astro will not include unused components in the build. Keep during development for reference.

risks.risk: Rapid slider dragging causes performance issues
risks.mitigation: Setting a CSS custom property is extremely cheap (no layout/paint). If jank observed, wrap in requestAnimationFrame. Expected to be unnecessary.

assumptions: color-mix() with calc() percentages is acceptable for the site's target browser baseline (2023+ browsers)
The shadow variables being binary-flipped (not smoothly interpolated) is visually acceptable
No automated test suite exists – verification is manual + build-success
The existing ThemeToggle.astro is not used outside of Nav.astro (verified by file listing)
The nav.css dark overlay (always dark, regardless of theme) intentionally stays dark and is not part of the interpolation scope
